description: Build phylogenetic tree of plant GUF1 paralogs showing chloroplastic vs mitochondrial clades
code:
import requests
import numpy as np
from collections import Counter

# Re-fetch all sequences in a single code block
accessions_to_fetch = [
    'A0A2U1PS28', 'A0A2U1PRZ2', 'A0A2U1PCN3',
    'Q9FNM5', 'Q9FLE4',
    'K7UFR2', 'A0A804QYQ1',    # Maize chloro, mito
    'I1KBR9', 'A0A0R0JP46',    # Soybean chloro, mito
    'B9F2U5', 'Q5VQ69',         # Rice chloro, mito
    'A0A072V0U5', 'G7KZV0',     # Medicago chloro, mito
    'A0A3Q7GS73', 'A0A3Q7G7B6', # Tomato chloro, mito
    'B9GHA6', 'B9IJU6',         # Poplar chloro, mito
    'A5B4D2', 'F6H5P1',         # Grape chloro, mito
]

labels = {
    'A0A2U1PS28': 'A.annua_TARGET',
    'A0A2U1PRZ2': 'A.annua_chloro',
    'A0A2U1PCN3': 'A.annua_mito',
    'Q9FNM5': 'Arab_chloro*',
    'Q9FLE4': 'Arab_mito*',
    'K7UFR2': 'Maize_chloro',
    'A0A804QYQ1': 'Maize_mito',
    'I1KBR9': 'Soybean_chloro',
    'A0A0R0JP46': 'Soybean_mito',
    'B9F2U5': 'Rice_chloro',
    'Q5VQ69': 'Rice_mito',
    'A0A072V0U5': 'Medicago_chloro',
    'G7KZV0': 'Medicago_mito',
    'A0A3Q7GS73': 'Tomato_chloro',
    'A0A3Q7G7B6': 'Tomato_mito',
    'B9GHA6': 'Poplar_chloro',
    'B9IJU6': 'Poplar_mito',
    'A5B4D2': 'Grape_chloro',
    'F6H5P1': 'Grape_mito',
}

seqs = {}
for acc in accessions_to_fetch:
    url = "https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/" + acc + ".fasta"
    resp = requests.get(url)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        lines = resp.text.strip().split('\n')
        seq = ''.join(lines[1:])
        seqs[acc] = seq

print("Fetched", len(seqs), "sequences")

# Compute pairwise k-mer Jaccard distances
k = 7
names = list(seqs.keys())
n = len(names)
dist_matrix = np.zeros((n, n))

for i in range(n):
    kmers_i = set(seqs[names[i]][j:j+k] for j in range(len(seqs[names[i]])-k+1))
    for j in range(i+1, n):
        kmers_j = set(seqs[names[j]][m:m+k] for m in range(len(seqs[names[j]])-k+1))
        jaccard = len(kmers_i & kmers_j) / max(1, len(kmers_i | kmers_j))
        dist = 1 - jaccard
        dist_matrix[i][j] = dist
        dist_matrix[j][i] = dist

# Simple neighbor-joining-like clustering using scipy
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import linkage, dendrogram
from scipy.spatial.distance import squareform

# Convert to condensed form
condensed = squareform(dist_matrix)

# UPGMA clustering
Z = linkage(condensed, method='average')

# Print the clustering result as text dendrogram
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 10))
leaf_labels = [labels.get(names[i], names[i]) for i in range(n)]

# Color chloroplastic entries green and mitochondrial red
def get_leaf_color(label):
    if 'chloro' in label.lower() or 'TARGET' in label:
        return 'green'
    elif 'mito' in label.lower():
        return 'red'
    return 'black'

dn = dendrogram(Z, labels=leaf_labels, orientation='left', ax=ax, leaf_font_size=11)

ax.set_title('Phylogenetic Clustering of Plant GUF1/EF-4 Paralogs\n(k=7 Jaccard distance, UPGMA)', fontsize=14)
ax.set_xlabel('K-mer Jaccard Distance', fontsize=12)

# Color the labels
ylbls = ax.get_yticklabels()
for lbl in ylbls:
    txt = lbl.get_text()
    if 'chloro' in txt.lower() or 'TARGET' in txt:
        lbl.set_color('darkgreen')
        if 'TARGET' in txt:
            lbl.set_fontweight('bold')
            lbl.set_fontsize(13)
    elif 'mito' in txt.lower():
        lbl.set_color('darkred')

# Add legend
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
legend_elements = [
    Patch(facecolor='darkgreen', label='Chloroplastic paralogs'),
    Patch(facecolor='darkred', label='Mitochondrial paralogs'),
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='lower right', fontsize=11)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('phylogenetic_tree.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("\nPhylogenetic tree saved.")

# Print distance from TARGET to all others
print("\n=== Distances from A0A2U1PS28 (TARGET) ===")
target_idx = names.index('A0A2U1PS28')
distances = []
for i in range(n):
    if i != target_idx:
        distances.append((labels.get(names[i], names[i]), dist_matrix[target_idx][i]))
distances.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
for lab, d in distances:
    marker = " <-- CLOSEST" if d == distances[0][1] else ""
    print("  " + lab.ljust(25) + ": " + str(round(d, 4)) + marker)
